Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows mild hypoglycemia and low HbA1c, with elevated creatinine indicating impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, ketonuria, glucosuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), suggesting possible nephrotic syndrome or diabetic nephropathy, though diabetes control appears tight or overtreated. No evidence of urinary infection. Recommend clinical correlation for renal and metabolic status, and review of diabetes management.
